title: pimelea imbricata var. major
intro: Pimelea imbricata var. major, also known as the 'Major Pimelea' or 'Hairy Pimelea', is a variety of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots. Traditionally, Pimelea imbricata var. major has been used by Indigenous Australians to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, with potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial activity.
